channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles restaurant named top place to eat in 2025
item.description: Yelp has once again released its annual rankings of the "Top 100 Places to Eat" in the nation, and a Los Angeles restaurant finds itself atop the list.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate confirms RFK Jr. as Health secretary; McConnell lone GOP dissenter
item.description: Longtime vaccine critic Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is now the nation’s top health official, after the Senate on Thursday voted almost entirely on party lines to confirm him atop a department of nearly 100,000 employees that run 13 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump's education secretary may be asked to dismantle the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: If Linda McMahon is confirmed as education secretary, President Donald Trump has said he wants the former wrestling executive to “put herself out of a job.”.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's pick for education chief says Congress needed to shut down department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon told senators she believes it would take an act of Congress to abolish the Education Department, saying the Trump administration wants to “do this right” and craft a plan that senators will support.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: At least 28 are hurt when a driver plows into a demonstration in Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring at least 28 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida man to be executed for 1997 double murder witnessed by toddler
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of murdering a husband and wife during a fishing trip at a remote farm while the couple's toddler looked on is scheduled to receive a lethal injection Thursday evening in the state's first execution this year.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Price fears for chocolatiers: Cocoa costs leave bitter taste for Valentine's Day
item.description: St. Valentine chocolates always seek to show how deep your love is. This year, it might just also show how deep your pockets are.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A free speech dispute over a New England town's pastry shop painting heads to trial
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A First Amendment dispute over a bright painting that shows sunbeams shining down on a mountain range made of sprinkle-covered chocolate and strawberry doughnuts, a blueberry muffin, a cinnamon roll and other pastries is scheduled for trial Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Johnson leaves door open to full-year funding stopgap
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) didn’t rule out a full-year stopgap to keep the government funded through the end of the fiscal year, as lawmakers struggle to strike a bipartisan deal. Pressed by reporters on the chances of a full-year stopgap, Johnson left the door open on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Court awaits Megan Boswell verdict from deliberating jury
item.description: SULLIVAN COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) - The jury in Megan Boswell’s trial will continue to deliberate Thursday. The jury was briefly called into the courtroom Thursday morning before being sent back to resume deliberating. On Wednesday, the prosecution and defense each gave their closing arguments.
item.dc:creator: Murry Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Egg prices hit record high of $4.95, will likely keep climbing
item.description: Of course, that is only the nationwide average. A carton of eggs can cost $10 or more in some places. And specialized varieties, such as organic and cage-free eggs, are even more expensive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Texas to execute man for 2004 murders of strip club manager and friend
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man who murdered his strip club manager and another man, then later prompted a massive lockdown of the state prison system when he used a cellphone smuggled into death row to threaten a lawmaker, was scheduled to be executed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge removes key legal hurdle for Trump’s plan to trim federal workforce with deferred resignations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday removed a key legal hurdle stalling President Donald Trump ’s plan to downsize the federal workforce with a deferred resignation program.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two crew members ejected from a Navy jet before it crashed Wednesday off the San Diego coast and were quickly rescued by a sportfishing boat, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House acknowledges disagreement with Jordan over relocating Palestinians
item.description: The White House on Wednesday acknowledged disagreement between President Trump and King Abdullah II of Jordon over Trump's pitch to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip while the U.S. takes over the territory.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of truck-driving serial killers at large, data suggests
item.description: Federal Bureau of Investigation data indicate that there are likely hundreds of homicidal truck drivers who have never been captured.
item.dc:creator: George Knapp, NewsNation

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baltimore sues Glock over handguns that can easily be converted to automatic weapons
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Baltimore is suing gunmaker Glock over design features that allow its handguns to be easily modified to become fully automatic weapons, which enables criminals to inflict more damage and makes the city more dangerous, attorneys argue in a complaint filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds singer R. Kelly's convictions, 30-year prison term
item.description: R. Kelly’s racketeering and sex trafficking convictions, along with a 30-year prison sentence, were upheld Wednesday by a federal appeals court that concluded the singer exploited his fame for over a quarter century to sexually abuse girls and young women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Italy prepares scenic, historic Olympic venues with games now one year away
item.description: This marks the first time Milan will host the Winter Olympics, with the opening ceremony scheduled at San Siro Stadium. The games will feature events in multiple locations, including hockey in a new arena in Milan and various alpine events in Cortina.
item.dc:creator: Matt Barnes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia school shooting suspect's father granted bond as victims share emotional statements in court
item.description: A Georgia judge granted Apalachee High School shooting suspect Colt Gray's father, Colin Gray, a $500,000 bond. Colin Gray has pleaded not guilty to 29 criminal counts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense sounds alarm on unidentified blood at student murders home
item.description: University of Idaho student murder suspect Bryan Kohberger's defense is arguing that two blood samples from unidentified males at the crime scene could clear him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump doubles down on plan to empty Gaza. This is what he has said and what's at stake
item.description: Behind U.S. President Donald Trump’s vows to turn Gaza into a “Riviera of the Middle East” lies a plan to forcibly drive a population from its land, rights groups say, warning it could be a war crime under international law.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Migrants stranded in Mexico try to restart life after Trump eliminates legal pathway to US
item.description: TIJUANA, Mexico (AP) — Margelis Rodriguez and her two children took selfies on their flight to Tijuana, showing off the T-shirts she had custom-made to mark what she expected to be her family's life-changing moment.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawaii court rules against insurance companies in Maui wildfire, allowing $4B settlement to proceed
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii’s Supreme Court ruled Monday that insurance companies can’t bring their own legal actions against those blamed for Maui’s catastrophic 2023 wildfire, allowing a $4 billion settlement that was on that the verge of collapse to proceed.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US judge keeps Trump plan to push out federal workers on hold
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's plan to downsize the federal workforce remains on hold after a courtroom hearing on Monday afternoon. It's the latest example of how the Republican president's ambitious plans have become ensnared in the judicial system. U.S. District Judge George O’Toole Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump warns of ending Israel-Hamas ceasefire if all hostages aren't returned
item.description: President Trump on Monday suggested he might end a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas if all hostages being held in Gaza are not released by the end of the week, warning that if the deal does not go through, “all hell is going to break out.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump accelerates Guantánamo Bay migrant directive: What to know
item.description: Trump administration officials are rapidly moving forward with President Trump’s directive to turn Guantánamo Bay into a facility that could hold up to 30,000 migrants who are being deported from the United States. Since announcing the move Jan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
